full_description:
A critical conceptual unit within the top term domain of the Exegesis. Mentions: 144. Referenced 144 times across the corpus. Buddha appears 144 times in the Exegesis corpus. From the Exegesis: "I used to think, How come God doesn't talk to us like he used to in the Bible? He does. If I were to go out into the world with a message, based on this 14 months of colloquy with God, it would be ..." Segment context: "Dick identifies his experience as a literal fulfillment of Biblical promise (Psalm 116) and a commissioning into the class of "prophets." Related to: Acts, Advent, After, Again, Although.

**Category:** Top Term

**Frequency:** 144 mentions.

**From the Exegesis:**
> I used to think, How come God doesn't talk to us like he used to in the Bible? He does. If I were to go out into the world with a message, based on this 14 months of colloquy with God, it would be that Santa Sophia will be reborn, she not having been acceptable before, as God told me; this would ...
> 136
that tell us about our own fate? He must have been born here and lived here; he must have died --preferably dramatically slain in a cruel and unfair way to emphasize his vulnerability-- and then not the immediate testimony right afterward of his resurrection, but rather the actual experience ...
> 161
The dream in TEARS signifies: the return of the King (i.e. the Second Advent of Christ).

The sentence, "St. Sophia will be reborn; she was not acceptable before," means exactly the same thing. Therefore, these sentences are in fact an explication of the dream; and the dream a visual graphic ...

**Key segment contexts:**
- (circa 1975-1976) Dick identifies his experience as a literal fulfillment of Biblical promise (Psalm 116) and a commissioning into the class of "prophets." He concludes that "Santa Sophia" (Holy Wisdom) is returning...
- (circa 1975-1976) Dick identifies the "Dead and Reborn God" experience as the emotional core of the Fish-Christian underground. He concludes that the universe is "inside-out" or "backward," with God (the Void) found...
- (circa 1975-1976) Dick refines the "Black Iron Prison" metaphor, describing a metal prison breached by "live signals" (roots) that teach slaves how to lift the siege. He identifies "James-James" (the Creator) as a p...

**Related terms:** Acts (related), Advent (related), After (related), Again (related), Although (related), Anamnesis (related)

evidence.text: I used to think, How come God doesn't talk to us like he used to in the Bible? He does. If I were to go out into the world with a message, based on this 14 months of colloquy with God, it would be that Santa Sophia will be reborn, she not having been acceptable before, as God told me; this would be the good news as it was given to me. So I would have a wonderful positive message, rather than one of gloom and doom. I sort of said this in my U.K. speech as well as the Arcade Comics piece, small as

136
that tell us about our own fate? He must have been born here and lived here; he must have died --preferably dramatically slain in a cruel and unfair way to emphasize his vulnerability-- and then not the immediate testimony right afterward of his resurrection, but rather the actual experience of Him during the mass of a contemporanous nature by each celebrant. I imagine the Fish-Christians ran around whispering excitedly to each other the thrilling news, "He lives!" based not on what 300 peop

161
The dream in TEARS signifies: the return of the King (i.e. the Second Advent of Christ).

The sentence, "St. Sophia will be reborn; she was not acceptable before," means exactly the same thing. Therefore, these sentences are in fact an explication of the dream; and the dream a visual graphic presentation of the sentences. Hence there is no need now to ask what the dream means; it has been explained. Anyhow, based on the Ancient of Days image in Daniel I was sure of this anyhow. But this remo

the EB article is talking about. Most important, this Christian underground had just lost the Savior but anticipated his imminent return; they were preparing for it. Put another way, their activities, which would include the overthrow of the tyranny, was in preparation for the Savior's return; it is all of one piece, with His return as the goal. The activity of God in history, then, would seem (from what I saw during that short interval of revelation) to be preparing the way of the Lord through

That was no subjective state; that was a perception of something real which others couldn't see; a set-ground gestalting. I discerned the doorway repeatedly; it was multilocated and authentic. Not omnipresent but multipresent. The Secret Kingdom, hidden.

I moment of fear touches me; did I then fail to pass through that gate and enter it? I think I passed through, because after seeing it (that was quite early along) I then had the holy waste & voice dreams, or visions, visionary trance experienc

It would appear from this that the first the spirit comes, from above, which is a sort of fire, and then there is paligge: new birth, the renewal of life, the resurrection. "He according to his mercy has saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing the Holy Spirit, which he shed (sic) on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Savior." (Titus 3:4-6.)

Dear Mr. Savoy:

Thank you so much for writing me. Yes indeed, I would like to take your study program. Perhaps you could have somebody write me as to costs, etc.

It has been nineteen months since my "conversion experience," as my priest calls it, began. It lasted, I discovered to my surprise, almost precisely one year, from March 18, 1974 to March 23, 1975.
